title: Extend-from-last-frame: capture the EXACT last frame (frame-accurate), not a native-seek drift
description: Follow-up from MPI-283 (frame-accurate video player). Extend / Create-New / 'Set as end frame' capture the source clip's last frame via MpiVideoViewer.captureSnapshot({time}) -> seeks the native <video> to time then captureFrameBlob() drawImage(<video>) (js/utils/video.js:47, js/components/Organisms/MpiVideoViewer/MpiVideoViewer.js:362, _captureLastFrameMedia in MpiGroupHistoryBlock.js:2165). COLOR IS FINE HERE: it draws the native <video>, which Chromium decodes with the correct YUV matrix -- it is NOT the WebCodecs canvas, so the vivid mis-convert bug does NOT apply to extraction (the MPI-283 handoff's color hypothesis was wrong; verified by reading captureFrameBlob). THE REAL GAP: <video>.currentTime is not frame-accurate by spec, so the seek can land on a drifted / interpolated frame near the end rather than the exact last frame -- the extended continuation then seeds from a slightly-wrong frame. FIX: route the last-frame grab through frameSink.getFrameCanvas(src, lastFrameIndex, effFps) (the same exact-decode path the player overlay uses) and toBlob that canvas; fall back to the current native captureFrameBlob only when the sink can't decode (canDecode gate -> null), exactly like the surface. Reuse frameSink; do not add a new decode path. Also decide lastFrameIndex the same way MpiVideoSurface.frameStep does (frameCount-1 for full range, round(trim.out*effFps) for a trim). NOTE: revisit AFTER the user's workflow-side experiment to drop the padded duplicate first frame (Wan/LTX 48+1) -- that may change what 'last frame' should be. Scope: small; single extraction site + frameSink reuse.
